source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/참고자료/참고 - 국내 기술문서/03. 기술문서 첨부자료/03 제품의 성능 및 안전을 확인하기 위한 자료/03 성능에 관한 자료/2. 사이버보안/7. A-CLA-HNXR1_Cybersecurity Labeling.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity labeling for the HnX-R1 Digital X-ray Imaging System, including security measures, communication ports, logging, SBOM, update process, backup and incident response.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory technical documentation by documenting cybersecurity warnings, precautions, configuration, assets, software components, support lifecycle, and response procedures for HnX-R1.
keyPoints: The scope is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System (HnX-R1).
The document states HnX-R1 is for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The system includes detector, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories, and HnVUE software, and does not include the X-ray generator.
Cybersecurity measures include endpoint protection, Windows Defender anti-malware, network connection allow list ACL, login by ID and password, and restricted unauthorized access.
Software or firmware updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after product collection by the manufacturer or service center.
If a cybersecurity threat is detected, users are instructed to disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact H&abyz service team.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity labeling
concepts.evidence: This is a document that has written warnings, precautions, and notices about software cyber risks.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

concepts.name: Data flow diagram assets
concepts.evidence: CRM-1~CRM-25(A1~A25): These assets are entities implemented in the console application and consist of a database, data manipulation process, detector, and communication interface between PACS/worklist servers and a GUI interface.

concepts.name: Communication ports
concepts.evidence: In this chapter, all ports of communication are mentioned.

concepts.name: Logging
concepts.evidence: The logs are updated in real time.

concepts.name: Software bill of materials
concepts.evidence: SBOM No Software name Component name Supplier Software level of support by manufacturer version Time stamp (Release date) EOS date Author Description Function

concepts.name: EOL and EOS
concepts.evidence: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product.

concepts.name: Software and firmware update process
concepts.evidence: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

concepts.name: Backup retention and recovery
concepts.evidence: When the device runs, it is automatically backed up.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity incident response
concepts.evidence: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Cybersecurity Labeling.
evidence.evidenceText: Cybersecurity Labeling

evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date August 06, 2025 Revision Description Initial

evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Jimin HAN RA team August 06, 2025; Reviewed by Kyungsoo LEE R&D Manager August 06, 2025; Approved by Namkyu HUR Quality Management Representative August 06, 2025

evidence.claim: The cybersecurity labeling scope is limited to HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: This cybersecurity labeling is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System (HnX-R1).

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital X-ray Imaging System and the model name is HnX-R1.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name Digital X-ray Imaging System Model name HnX-R1

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 detector reference numbers include G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, G1717FWP, A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector 1 (G series) G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, G1717FWP Detector 2 (2/3G series) A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is listed as the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-R1 system does not include the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1 wired/wireless digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Other Accessories and software for image acquisition and image processing and does not include the X-ray generator.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 uses HnVUE as console software for image acquisition and image processing.
evidence.evidenceText: The name of the image acquisition and image processing software (Console software) is HnVUE.

evidence.claim: Captured images are saved in DICOM and sent to PACS.
evidence.evidenceText: saved in DICOM file, a standard for medical imaging. The captured images are sent to the Picture Archiving & Communication System (PACS) server

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity risks were addressed as part of device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adult and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended PATIENT population Adult and children except infants and pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended user profile includes radiologists and medical imaging specialists with healthcare professional training.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: Completed a healthcare professional training; Knowledge: Qualified as a radiologist; Experience: Radiologist, medical imaging specialists

evidence.claim: The labeling section covers security measures, maintenance, and best practices for end users to manage cybersecurity risks.
evidence.evidenceText: This section will outline HnX-R1’s security measures, maintenance, and best practices for the end users to manage cybersecurity risks associated with the modules and the use of the software.

evidence.claim: The console application assets include database, data manipulation process, detector, PACS/worklist communication interface, and GUI interface.
evidence.evidenceText: CRM-1~CRM-25(A1~A25): These assets are entities implemented in the console application and consist of a database, data manipulation process, detector, and communication interface between PACS/worklist servers and a GUI interface

evidence.claim: The detector assets include firmware processes, storage, and communication interfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: A26~A32: These assets are entities implemented in the detector inside and consist of a database, data manipulation process, interface for the console program to communication its image data.

evidence.claim: Endpoint protection includes Wi-Fi communication referenced to cybersecurity test report EBB-2025-000109.
evidence.evidenceText: End point protection Wi-FI communication : Refer to Cybersecurity test report (Report no. EBB-2025-000109)

evidence.claim: Anti-malware is listed as Windows Defender.
evidence.evidenceText: Anti-malware : window defender

evidence.claim: Firewall rules use a network connection allow list ACL.
evidence.evidenceText: Firewall / Firewall rules : Network connection white list(ACL)

evidence.claim: Authorized software access is granted by logging in using an ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Access to the software can be granted by logging in using an ID and password.

evidence.claim: Unauthorized users cannot log in and software installations may only be installed by an authorized person.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized users can't login and have limited access to the software. Software installations may only be installed by an authorized person.

evidence.claim: The Tether Connector is enabled for both incoming and outgoing TCP/IP socket communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Tether Connector Enable Both TCP/IP Socket communication

evidence.claim: The Antenna is enabled for Wi-Fi communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Antenna Enable Wifi Antenna Antenna for Wifi Communication

evidence.claim: USB-C is disabled.
evidence.evidenceText: USB-C Disable NA

evidence.claim: Logs are checked through HnVue by clicking View log in the main window menu.
evidence.evidenceText: How to check logs in real-time communication : Run HnVue Click menu in the main windows” View log”

evidence.claim: Log files are saved in .txt format and hidden to prevent access by general users.
evidence.evidenceText: The log file is saved in a .txt format and is hidden to prevent access by general users

evidence.claim: The log file is used through a log viewer instead of IDS or SIEM automated analysis software.
evidence.evidenceText: The log file is utilized through a log viewer instead of automated analysis software IDS , SIEM

evidence.claim: The SBOM lists HnVue and Detector software components, suppliers, support status, versions, release dates, EOS dates, authors, descriptions, and functions.
evidence.evidenceText: SBOM No Software name Component name Supplier Software level of support by manufacturer version Time stamp (Release date) EOS date Author Description Function

evidence.claim: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS; end date is determined by software change or a 10-year software life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product. The end date is determined by the software change or the software life cycle (10 years).

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

evidence.claim: When the device runs, setup files and log files are automatically backed up.
evidence.evidenceText: When the device runs, it is automatically backed up. Set up Files, Log Files

evidence.claim: Unauthorized individuals cannot perform backups.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized individuals cannot perform backups

evidence.claim: If setup files do not exist or cannot be read, the system automatically restores the latest backed-up files.
evidence.evidenceText: If Setup Files does not exist or cannot be read, it automatically restores the latest backed-up files.

evidence.claim: For cybersecurity threats, the instructed response is to disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact H&abyz service team.
evidence.evidenceText: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is supplied only by metadata as authoritySignal MFDS/domestic and is not explicitly stated in the document body.
Several security configuration items are marked N/A and defer to Cybersecurity test report EBB-2025-000109, which is not included in this source text.
SBOM release date formatting is inconsistent and includes OCR or transcription artifacts such as unkonown, window defender, and split version/date fields.
No explicit regulatory standard is listed; DICOM is included only because the text calls it a standard for medical imaging.
